raw_consumer_statement:
XX/XX/XXXX Contacted AMEX upon receive of email change notice. A new unauthorized user was added to my account. Spoke to fraud prevention department and they cancel my existing card including the newly added fraudulent account. 25 minutes were spent on call to resolve issues. 

XX/XX/XXXX Fraudulent transactions started to appear on my account despite it was supposed to be closed on the XXXX on XXXX. In addition, another unauthorized user was added to my account. AMEX fraud prevention department claimed they will remove unauthorized charges and close the newly added fraudulent account. A total of 23 minutes spent on call to resolve issue. 

XX/XX/XXXX Contacted AMEX with issues adding my new card to XXXX XXXX and follow up on unauthorized charge removal. The new card was not able to be added to XXXX XXXXXXXX hence a new card had to be issued. I was assured that unauthorized charges would be removed and will not be held liable. Spent a total of 47 minutes and 4 calls speaking to multiple representatives to resolve issues. 

XX/XX/XXXX Fraudulent transactions on the second unauthorized account started to appear on my account. That account was supposed to be closed on XX/XX/XXXX. I was asked on the call to confirm the unauthorized users for reason I can not fathom. The two unauthorized account were supposed to be closed on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX calls respectively. Spent 33 minutes on call trying to get my account secured and removing unauthorized charges. 

XX/XX/XXXX All the fraudulent transactions went from pending to post status on my account. Spent 11 minutes on call asking representatives why they have yet to execute what I was told on previous calls. I may have to pay over XXXX XXXX out of pocket first to prevent fees and impact to credit profile.
